informal_statement.text: Sort a mutable slice of integers in nondecreasing order using insertion sort. The function mutates its input in place and returns nothing.
required_properties: the resulting slice is sorted in nondecreasing order
the resulting slice is a permutation of the original slice
the function terminates on every input

semantic_units.description: Sortedness: the output list is sorted in nondecreasing order.
Permutation: the output is a permutation of the input (every element preserved, counts preserved).
common_failure_modes: asserting set equality without counting duplicates

semantic_units.description: Stability is NOT required and should not be asserted.

reference_obligations.nl_gloss: The output is sorted in nondecreasing order.
The output is a permutation of the input.

generated_obligations.nl_gloss: No explicit preconditions are required by the implementation; it works for any mutable slice/array of i32 (Lean Int).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (i : Nat), i < arr.size → (let j0 := i; ∃ j, j ≤ j0 ∧ (j = 0 ∨ ¬(arr.get! (j-1) > arr.get! j)))
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The inner while-loop terminates because j decreases strictly on each swap and is bounded below by 0.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (i : Nat), i ≤ arr.size →
  let a := arr in
  (Sorted (a.subarray 0 i)) ∧ (Permutation a (a))
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: After completing the outer loop for index i (i.e., before processing i), the prefix arr[0..i) is sorted in ascending order. The algorithm only swaps elements, so the multiset of elements is preserved (permutation).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (i j : Nat), i < arr.size → j ≤ i →
  (Permutation arr arr) ∧
  (Sorted (arr.subarray 0 j)) ∧
  (∀ k, k < j → arr[k] ≤ arr[j])
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: During the inner while-loop for a fixed i, the portion before j remains sorted, and all elements in that prefix are ≤ the current element at position j. Swaps only move the current element left while maintaining these properties.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (i j : Nat), i < arr.size → j > 0 → j ≤ i →
  arr[j-1] > arr[j] →
  (let arr' := arr.swap (j-1) j in
   Sorted (arr'.subarray 0 (j-1)) ∧
   (∀ k, k < (j-1) → arr'[k] ≤ arr'[j-1]) ∧
   Permutation arr arr')
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the while-condition holds (arr[j-1] > arr[j]), swapping positions j-1 and j preserves the sortedness of the already-processed prefix and maintains the relationship needed for the current element to continue moving left.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int),
  let a0 := arr in
  insertion_sort a0;
  Sorted a0
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: After the algorithm finishes, the entire array is sorted in ascending order.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int),
  let a0 := arr in
  insertion_sort a0;
  Permutation a0 arr
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: After the algorithm finishes, the output array is a permutation of the input array (it only uses swaps, so it preserves the multiset of elements).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int),
  let a0 := arr in
  insertion_sort a0;
  (Sorted a0) ∧ (Permutation a0 arr) →
  a0 = sort arr
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Combining sortedness and permutation characterizes the result as the standard sorted version of the input (i.e., insertion sort computes the ascending sort).
